name: Amish Peanut Butter Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat together the powdered sugar and peanut butter until the mixture is crumbly; set aside.
2. In a large, heavy saucepan, combine 2/3 cup sugar and 2 cups milk; heat to scalding or until bubbles start to form on the bottom. Do not let it boil.
3. Meanwhile, in a medium bowl, beat the egg yolks to mix; blend in 6 tablespoons cornstarch, 3 tablespoons flour, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Stir to make a paste.
4. Whisk in the remaining 2 cups cold milk, whisking until the mixture is smooth.
5. Pour in some of the hot milk mixture, stirring to combine.
6. Add the mixture from the bowl to the milk in the saucepan.
7. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture bubbles up in the center.
8. Add the butter and 1 teaspoon vanilla. Remove from heat and let custard cool.
9. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
10. Sprinkle 2/3 of the crumbly peanut butter mixture into the bottom of the baked (and cooled) pastry shell.
11. Pour the cooled custard mixture over the top.
12. In a large mixer bowl, place the egg whites, cream of tartar, and remaining teaspoon vanilla. Beat until stiff peaks form.
13. Gradually, while beating, add the 4 tablespoons remaining sugar and 3 tablespoons remaining cornstarch. Continue beating until the whites are very thick and glossy.
14. Spread on top of pie; sprinkle the remaining peanut butter mixture on top.
15. Bake for 1- to 15 minutes, watching carefully, or until the meringue is golden brown. Cool.
